task_completion.description: Open the original floorplan.dxf file in the CAD application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAD application (e.g., LibreCAD) is open, displaying a complex architectural floor plan. The window title or active tab shows 'floorplan.dxf'.
The Layer List or Layer Settings dialog shows a layer explicitly named 'Dimensions'.

task_completion.description: Set the 'Dimensions' layer color to red
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Layer List or Layer Settings, the color indicator/icon next to the 'Dimensions' layer is red.
The Layer List or Layer Settings dialog shows a layer explicitly named 'Notes'.

task_completion.description: Set the 'Notes' layer color to blue
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Layer List or Layer Settings, the color indicator/icon next to the 'Notes' layer is blue.
A 'Save As' dialog is visible with the filename 'floorplan_layers.dxf', or the application title bar/tab updates to show 'floorplan_layers.dxf' after saving.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with CAD application menus, layer widgets, and dialog boxes, rather than using a terminal or text editor to modify the DXF file directly.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual provided floor plan rather than a blank file
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing canvas displays a dense, complex architectural floor plan with walls, doors, and annotations, matching the original file's contents.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent writing or executing a Python script (e.g., using ezdxf) in the terminal to silently add the layers and save the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original file 'floorplan.dxf' is a real, complex architectural drawing containing 24 existing layers (e.g., A-WALL, A-GARAGE-DOOR) and numerous entities (lines, dimensions, text). The VLM should expect to see a detailed floor plan in the CAD application, not a blank or simple drawing. The final file must be saved as 'floorplan_layers.dxf'.
